name: Kenny Drew
abstract: Kenneth Sidney "Kenny" Drew (August 28, 1928 – August 4, 1993) was an American-Danish jazz pianist.

about_quotes.speaker: DeFranco Buddy
about_quotes.evidence: the house band was Art Blakey, Kenny Drew, and Eugene Wright... we became a bona fide traveling group
about_quotes.doc_title: DeFranco Buddy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: But he got Kenny Drew, pianist; Niels Ørsted Pedersen, bass; Arthur Taylor, drums; Dizzy Reece, trumpet; and Dexter and myself. We didn’t have a rehearsal before the record date. We went right in the studio.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: The 21st of June, I did a concert in Joinville, a suburb of Paris with Benny Carter, Ben Webster, Kenny Drew, Art Taylor, Michel Sarbadi, Johnny Griffin, The Golden Gate Quartet.

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: Anyway, that was with Kenny Drew, and no more there. Art Taylor. No more there. Wilbur Ware... And Pepper Adams... I played well on that record.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Alvin Queen
about_quotes.evidence: The easiest one, I was with Kenny Drew. I was with Kenny for about four or five years I believe.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alvin Queen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 23, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: That’s where I would play dances, those ten dollar gigs, with Sonny Rollins and Kenny Drew.
about_quotes.doc_title: Percy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
